description: Look up current documentation for a library or API via ${server_name}.
template_md:
# /${server_name}-docs <library>

Fetch up-to-date documentation for the named library from ${server_url}.

## When to use

- Before recommending an external API or pattern.
- When user asks about a specific framework, SDK, CLI tool, or cloud service.

## Usage

1. Call `mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` with the library name.
2. Call `mcp__context7__query-docs` with the resolved id and the question.

_Server: ${server_name} — ${server_url}_

description: Semantic search across internal PDAP product documentation via ${server_name}.
template_md:
# /${server_name}-search <query>

Query internal PDAP product docs at ${server_url}.

## When to use

- Internal-only product knowledge questions.
- TFS bug research and RSPDN context lookup.

## Usage

1. `mcp__pdap-docs__search` with the user query.
2. Cite returned doc IDs verbatim — do not paraphrase IDs.

_Server: ${server_name} — ${server_url}_

description: Route an implementation task to the correct pipeline via ${server_name}.
template_md:
# /${server_name}-route <description>

Route the described task through the orchestrator pipeline classifier.

## When to use

- Before any non-trivial implementation (>3 files or any architecture change).
- Before /run, /phase, /finish skill invocations.

## Usage

1. `mcp__orchestrator__route_task(description=...)`.
2. Follow the returned `pipeline_type` assignment exactly — do not improvise.

_Server: ${server_name}_

# /${server_name}-review <files>

Run cross-model code review on the listed files.

## When to use

- After writing or modifying non-trivial code.
- Before any commit that touches >3 files.

## Usage

1. `mcp__pal__codereview` with the file list and review focus.
2. Inspect findings; HALT on any finding at or above CLAUDE_GATE_MIN_BLOCKING_SEVERITY.
3. Use `mcp__pal__challenge` to dispute false positives — never silently downgrade.

_Server: ${server_name}_

description: Drive an SAP transaction code from ${server_name}.
template_md:
# /${server_name}-tx <code>

Navigate the active SAP GUI session to the named transaction code.

## When to use

- Reading SE80 / SE16N / SE38 / SM37 screens during ABAP investigation.
- Capturing field values from SAP master data screens.

## Usage

1. `mcp__sap-gui-control__navigate` with the transaction code.
2. `mcp__sap-gui-control__snapshot` to capture screen state.

_Server: ${server_name}_
